{\n106\t\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n107\t\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n108\t\t\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n109\t\t\tsuggestions = =============================================================================\n128\t// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON VALIDATION SCENARIOS\n129\t// =============================================================================\n130\t\n131\t// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n132\t//\n133\t// Parameters:\n134\t//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n135\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n136\t//   - context: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"GET /api/users\")\n137\t//\n138\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n139\t//\n140\t// Example usage:\n141\t//\n142\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n143\t//\t// Output:\n144\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n145\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n146\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not exists\n151\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n152\tfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n153\t\treturn FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n193\t//\n194\t// Parameters:\n195\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n196\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n197\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n198\t//\n199\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n200\t//\n201\t// Example usage:\n202\t//\n203\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\")\n204\t//\t// Output:\n205\t//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n206\t//\t//   Expected: 4xx (400-499)\n207\t//\t//   Actual:   200\n208\t//\t//   Context:  error response check\n209\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n210\t//\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\n211\t//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n212\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n213\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n214\t\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n215\t\n216\t\t// Build range info string\n217\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n218\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n219\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\tdetails := []string{}\n223\t\tif err != nil {\n224\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n225\t\t} else {\n226\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n227\t\t}\n228\t\n229\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n230\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, {\n237\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n238\t\t}\n239\t\n240\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n241\t}\n242\t\n243\t// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\n244\t//\n245\t// Parameters:\n246\t//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \"application/json\")\n247\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\n248\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n249\t//\n250\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n251\t//\n252\t// Example usage:\n253\t//\n254\t//\terr :=     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\n263\t//\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\n264\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n273\t//\n274\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n275\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n276\t// your specific requirements.\n277\t//\n278\t// Parameters:\n279\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n280\t//   - expected: The expected value\n281\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n282\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n283\t//\n284\t// Example usage:\n285\t//\n286\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n287\t//\t    \"custom_field\",\n288\t//\t    \"required_value\",\n289\t//\t    \"actual_value\",\n290\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n291\t//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n292\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n293\t//\t)\n294\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n295\t\tvalidationType string,\n296\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n297\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n298\t) ValidationError {\n299\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n300\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n301\t\t\topt(config)\n302\t\t}\n303\t\n304\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n305\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n306\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n307\t\n308\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n309\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n310\t\t}\n311\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n312\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n313\t\t}\n314\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n315\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n316\t\t}\n317\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n318\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n319\t\t}\n320\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n321\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n322\t\t}\n323\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n324\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n325\t\t}\n326\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n327\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n328\t\t}\n329\t\n330\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// =============================================================================\n334\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n335\t// =============================================================================\n336\t\n337\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\n338\ttype FormatConfig struct []string\n345\t\tSuggestions        []string\n346\t\t// Category-aware and severity support\n347\t\tSeverityOverride   ErrorSeverity\n348\t\tCategoryHint       ErrorCategory\n349\t\tStatusCode         int\n350\t\tTimeout            int\n351\t\tSecurityContext    string\n352\t}\n353\t\n354\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\n355\ttype FormatOption {\n360\t\t\tc.Context = context\n361\t\t}\n362\t}\n363\t\n364\t// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\n365\tfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\n373\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n374\t\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n375\t\t}\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that info\n389\t\t}\n390\t}\n391\t\n392\t// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\n393\tfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n394\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n395\t\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n396\t\t}\n397\t}\n398\t\n399\t// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions...)\n403\t\t}\n404\t}\n405\t\n406\t// WithSeverityOverride creates a FormatOption that overrides the default severity level.\n407\t// Use this when you want to specify a custom severity instead of using the error type's default.\n408\t//\n409\t// Example usage:\n410\t//\n411\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n412\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n413\t//\t    \"Field is required\",\n414\t//\t    \"email\",\n415\t//\t    validate.WithSeverityOverride(validate.SeverityCritical),\n416\t//\t)\n417\tfunc WithSeverityOverride(severity ErrorSeverity) FormatOption validate.WithCategoryHint(validate.CategoryValidation),\n433\t//\t)\n434\tfunc WithCategoryHint(category ErrorCategory) FormatOption {\n435\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n436\t\t\tc.CategoryHint = information in the formatted message.\n442\t//\n443\t// Example usage:\n444\t//\n445\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n446\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n447\t//\t    \"Resource not found\",\n448\t//\t    \"endpoint\",\n449\t//\t    validate.WithStatusCode(404),\n450\t//\t)\n451\tfunc WithStatusCode(statusCode int) FormatOption {\n452\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n453\t\t\tc.StatusCode = statusCode\n454\t\t}\n455\t}\n456\t\n457\t// WithTimeout creates a FormatOption that sets the timeout for performance errors.\n458\t// Use this with performance-related errors to include timeout information in the formatted message.\n459\t//\n460\t// Example usage:\n461\t//\n462\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n463\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n464\t//\t    \"Request timed out\",\n465\t//\t    \"api_response\",\n466\t//\t    validate.WithTimeout(5000),\n467\t//\t)\n468\tfunc WithTimeout(timeout int) FormatOption {\n469\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n470\t\t\tc.Timeout = timeout\n471\t\t}\n472\t}\n473\t\n474\t// WithSecurityContext creates a FormatOption that sets the security context for security errors.\n475\t// Use this with security-related errors to include security context in the formatted message.\n476\t//\n477\t// Example usage:\n478\t//\n479\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n480\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n481\t//\t    \"Invalid credentials\",\n482\t//\t    \"Authorization\",\n483\t//\t    validate.WithSecurityContext(\"authentication\"),\n484\t//\t)\n485\tfunc WithSecurityContext(securityContext string) FormatOption {\n486\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n487\t\t\tc.SecurityContext = securityContext\n488\t\t}\n489\t}\n490\t\n491\t\n492\t// =============================================================================\n493\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n494\t// =============================================================================\n495\t\n496\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n497\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n498\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n499\t\tif err != nil {\n500\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n501\t\t}\n502\t\n503\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n504\t\tif err != nil {\n505\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n506\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n507\t\t}\n508\t\n509\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n510\t}\n511\t\n512\t// =============================================================================\n513\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n514\t// =============================================================================\n515\t\n516\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n517\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n518\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n519\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n520\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n521\t}\n522\t\n523\t// global tracker instance\n524\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n525\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n526\t}\n527\t\n528\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n529\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n530\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n531\t//\n532\t// Parameters:\n533\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n534\t//\n535\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n536\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n537\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n538\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n539\t\n540\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n541\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n542\t}\n543\t\n544\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n545\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n546\t// configuration issues.\n547\t//\n548\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n549\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n550\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n551\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n552\t\n553\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n554\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n555\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n556\t\t}\n557\t\treturn result\n558\t}\n559\t\n560\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n561\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n562\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n563\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n564\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n565\t\n566\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n567\t}\n568\t\n569\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n570\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n571\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n572\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n573\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n574\t\n575\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n576\t}\n577\t\n578\t// =============================================================================\n579\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n580\t// =============================================================================\n581\t\n582\t// FormatError creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum.\n583\t// This is the primary error formatting function that provides type-safe error classification.\n584\t//\n585\t// This function uses ErrorType enum for consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n586\t// For backward compatibility with string-based error types, use FormatErrorString.\n587\t//\n588\t// ErrorType to Message Mapping:\n589\t// The ErrorType enum provides type-safe error classification with the following mappings:\n590\t//   - ErrTypeRequired (required): Required field is missing or empty\n591\t//   - ErrTypeFormat (format): Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n592\t//   - ErrTypeRange (range): Value is outside acceptable numeric range (min/max)\n593\t//   - ErrTypeLength (length): String length or collection size is invalid\n594\t//   - ErrTypeType (type): Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n595\t//   - ErrTypeValue (value): Value is invalid for domain-specific reasons\n596\t//   - ErrTypeDuplicate (duplicate): Duplicate value detected\n597\t//   - ErrTypeConflict (conflict): Conflict with existing values or constraints\n598\t//   - ErrTypeUnknown (unknown): Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n599\t//\n600\t// Parameters:\n601\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n602\t//   - message: The error message content\n603\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n604\t//\n605\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure: \"[errorType] fieldName: message\"\n606\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n607\t//\n608\t// Example usage:\n609\t//\n610\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n611\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n612\t//\n613\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n614\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n615\t//\n616\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n617\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n618\t//\n619\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"\", \"email\")\n620\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: email validation failed\" (empty message fallback)\n621\tfunc FormatError(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n622\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n623\t\t// This converts the typed ErrorType enum to its string representation\n624\t\t// for consistent message formatting across the codebase\n625\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n626\t\n627\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n628\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n629\t\n630\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n631\t\tif message == \"\" {\n632\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n633\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n634\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n635\t\t\t} else {\n636\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n637\t\t\t}\n638\t\t}\n639\t\n640\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n641\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n642\t}\n643\t\n644\t// FormatErrorString creates a formatted error message using string error type.\n645\t// This function provides backward compatibility for code that uses string-based error types.\n646\t//\n647\t// This function validates string error types against the ErrorType enum for type-safe\n648\t// error classification while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value.\n649\t//\n650\t// Error Type Validation:\n651\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n652\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n653\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n654\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n655\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n656\t//\n657\t// Parameters:\n658\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error as a string (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n659\t//   - message: The error message content\n660\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (variadic parameter)\n661\t//\n662\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n663\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n664\t//\n665\t// Example usage:\n666\t//\n667\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n668\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n669\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n670\t//\n671\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\")\n672\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n673\t//\n674\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n675\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n676\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n677\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n678\t//\n679\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n680\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n681\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n682\tfunc FormatErrorString(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n683\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args first\n684\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n685\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n686\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n687\t\t}\n688\t\n689\t\t// Check if errorType is whitespace-only (not truly empty)\n690\t\t// Whitespace-only error types should be tracked as invalid\n691\t\t// because they are likely user errors, not intentional defaults\n692\t\toriginalHasContent := errorType != \"\"\n693\t\ttrimmedType := strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n694\t\tisWhitespaceOnly := originalHasContent && trimmedType == \"\"\n695\t\n696\t\t// Track whitespace-only error types as invalid\n697\t\tif isWhitespaceOnly {\n698\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n699\t\t}\n700\t\n701\t\t// Trim whitespace from errorType before validation\n702\t\terrorType = trimmedType\n703\t\n704\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n705\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n706\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n707\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n708\t\n709\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n710\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the trimmed errorType wasn't \"unknown\" (case-insensitive),\n711\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n712\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && !strings.EqualFold(errorType, \"unknown\") {\n713\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n714\t\t}\n715\t\n716\t\t// Trim whitespace from message and fieldName before checking\n717\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n718\t\tfieldNameStr = strings.TrimSpace(fieldNameStr)\n719\t\n720\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n721\t\tif message == \"\" {\n722\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided (after trimming)\n723\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n724\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n725\t\t\t} else {\n726\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n727\t\t\t}\n728\t\t}\n729\t\n730\t\t// Handle empty errorType (after trimming) - use fallback\n731\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n732\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n733\t\t}\n734\t\n735\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the processed error type string\n736\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n737\t}\n738\t\n739\t// FormatErrorWithType is an alias for FormatError for backward compatibility.\n740\t// This function calls FormatError with the same parameters.\n741\t//\n742\t// Deprecated: Use FormatError directly with ErrorType enum parameter instead.\n743\t// This function is maintained for backward compatibility with existing code.\n744\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n745\t\treturn FormatError(errorType, message, fieldName)\n746\t}\n747\t\n748\t// =============================================================================\n749\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n750\t// =============================================================================\n751\t\n752\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n753\ttype QuoteStyle string\n754\t\n755\tconst (\n756\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n757\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n758\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n759\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n760\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n761\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n762\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n763\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n764\t)\n765\t\n766\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n767\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n768\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n769\t\tprefix     string\n770\t}\n771\t\n772\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n773\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n774\t\n775\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n776\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n777\t//\n778\t// Example usage:\n779\t//\n780\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n781\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n782\t//\n783\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n784\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n785\tfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n786\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n787\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n788\t\t}\n789\t}\n790\t\n791\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n792\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n793\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n794\t//\n795\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n796\t//\n797\t// Example usage:\n798\t//\n799\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n800\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n801\t//\n802\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n803\t//\t// Returns: request.email\n804\t//\n805\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n806\t//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\n807\tfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n808\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n809\t\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n810\t\t}\n811\t}\n812\t\n813\t// =============================================================================\n814\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n815\t// =============================================================================\n816\t\n817\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n818\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n819\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n820\t//\n821\t// Parameters:\n822\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n823\t//\n824\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n825\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n826\t//\n827\t// Example usage:\n828\t//\n829\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n830\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n831\t//\n832\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n833\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n834\t//\n835\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n836\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n837\t//\n838\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n839\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n840\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n841\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n842\t\n843\t\t// Handle empty path\n844\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n845\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n846\t\t}\n847\t\n848\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n849\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n850\t}\n851\t\n852\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n853\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n854\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n855\t\tif path == \"\" {\n856\t\t\treturn \"\"\n857\t\t}\n858\t\n859\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n860\t\tvar result []string\n861\t\n862\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n863\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n864\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n865\t\t\t\tcontinue\n866\t\t\t}\n867\t\n868\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n869\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n870\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n871\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n872\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n873\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n874\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n875\t\t\t\t} else {\n876\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n877\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n878\t\t\t\t}\n879\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n880\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n881\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n882\t\t\t} else {\n883\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n884\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n885\t\t\t}\n886\t\t}\n887\t\n888\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n889\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n890\t\t}\n891\t\n892\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n893\t}\n894\t\n895\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n896\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n897\t\tif s == \"\" {\n898\t\t\treturn false\n899\t\t}\n900\t\n901\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n902\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n903\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n904\t\t\t\treturn false\n905\t\t\t}\n906\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n907\t\t}\n908\t\n909\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n910\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n911\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n912\t\t\t\treturn false\n913\t\t\t}\n914\t\t}\n915\t\n916\t\treturn true\n917\t}\n918\t\n919\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n920\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n921\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n922\t}\n923\t\n924\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n925\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n926\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n927\t// for field names through variadic options.\n928\t//\n929\t// Parameters:\n930\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n931\t//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n932\t//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n933\t//\n934\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n935\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n936\t//\n937\t// Example usage:\n938\t//\n939\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n940\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n941\t//\n942\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n943\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n944\t//\n945\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n946\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n947\t//\n948\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n949\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n950\t//\n951\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n952\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n953\t//\n954\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n955\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n956\t//\n957\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n958\t//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n959\t//\n960\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n961\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n962\t//\n963\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n964\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n965\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n966\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n967\t\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n968\t\n969\t\t// Apply options\n970\t\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n971\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n972\t\t\topt(config)\n973\t\t}\n974\t\n975\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n976\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n977\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n978\t\t}\n979\t\n980\t\t// Handle empty field path\n981\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n982\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n983\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n984\t\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n985\t\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n986\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n987\t\t\t\t}\n988\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n989\t\t\t}\n990\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n991\t\t}\n992\t\n993\t\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n994\t\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n995\t\n996\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n997\t\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n998\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n999\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1000\t\t\t}\n1001\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1002\t\t}\n1003\t\n1004\t\t// Apply quote style if specified\n1005\t\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n1006\t\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n1007\t\t}\n1008\t\n1009\t\t// Add prefix if provided\n1010\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1011\t\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n1012\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1013\t\n1014\t\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n1015\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1016\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1017\t\t\t}\n1018\t\n1019\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n1020\t\t}\n1021\t\n1022\t\treturn \"'users'[0].'email'\").\n1027\tfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n1028\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n1029\t\t\treturn path\n1030\t\t}\n1031\t\n1032\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n1033\t\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n1034\t\tinBrackets := false\n1035\t\n1036\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n1037\t\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n1038\t\n1039\t\t\tswitch {\n1040\t\t\tcase r == '.':\n1041\t\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n1042\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1043\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1044\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1045\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1046\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1047\t\t\t\t}\n1048\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('.')\n1049\t\n1050\t\t\tcase r == '[':\n1051\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n1052\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1053\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1054\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1055\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1056\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1057\t\t\t\t}\n1058\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n1059\t\t\t\tinBrackets = true\n1060\t\n1061\t\t\tcase r == ']':\n1062\t\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n1063\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n1064\t\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n1065\t\n1066\t\t\tdefault:\n1067\t\t\t\t// Only accumulate field name characters when NOT inside brackets\n1068\t\t\t\tif !inBrackets {\n1069\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.WriteRune(r)\n1070\t\t\t\t} else {\n1071\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(r)\n1072\t\t\t\t}\n1073\t\t\t}\n1074\t\t}\n1075\t\n1076\t\t// Don't forget the last field name\n1077\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1078\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1079\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1080\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1081\t\t}\n1082\t\n1083\t\treturn result.String()\n1084\t}\n1085\t\n1086\t// =============================================================================\n1087\t// COMPREHENSIVE VALIDATION ERROR FORMATTING WITH DETAILS\n1088\t//
