responses and other data.\n2\tpackage validate\n3\t\n4\timport (\n5\t\t\"encoding/json\"\n6\t\t\"fmt\"\n7\t\t\"io\"\n8\t\t\"net/http\"\n9\t\t\"regexp\"\n10\t\t\"strings\"\n11\t)\n12\t\n13\t// HTTPStatusCodeIsValid checks if an HTTP response's status code matches one or more expected codes.\n14\t// It supports both a single status code and a slice of valid status codes, making it useful for\n15\t// validating API responses where multiple status codes might indicate success (e.g., 200 OK and 206 Partial Content).\n16\t//\n17\t// The expected parameter can be:\n18\t//   - A single int (e.g., 200)\n19\t//   - A slice of ints (e.g., []int{200, 201, 204})\n20\t//\n21\t// Returns true if the response status code matches any of the expected codes, false otherwise.\n22\t//\n23\t// Example usage:\n24\t//\n25\t//\tsingleCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 200)\n26\t//\tmultipleCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, []int{200, 201, 204})\n27\t//\tnoContentCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 204)\n28\tfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}) bool {\n29\t\tif resp == nil {\n30\t\t\treturn false\n31\t\t}\n32\t\n33\t\tstatusCode := resp.StatusCode\n34\t\n35\t\tswitch v := expected.(type) {\n36\t\tcase int:\n37\t\t\treturn statusCode == v\n38\t\tcase []int:\n39\t\t\tfor _, code := range v {\n40\t\t\t\tif statusCode == code {\n41\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n42\t\t\t\t}\n43\t\t\t}\n44\t\t\treturn false\n45\t\tdefault:\n46\t\t\treturn false\n47\t\t}\n48\t}\n49\t\n50\t// HTTPStatusCodeIsError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates an error (4xx or 5xx).\n51\t//\n52\t// Returns true if the response status code is in the 400-599 range, false otherwise.\n53\t//\n54\t// Example usage:\n55\t//\n56\t//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsError(response) {\n57\t//\t    // Handle error response\n58\t//\t}\n59\tfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n60\t\tif resp == nil {\n61\t\t\treturn false\n62\t\t}\n63\t\n64\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 600\n65\t}\n66\t\n67\t// HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates a client error (4xx).\n68\t//\n69\t// Returns true if the response status code is in the 400-499 range, false otherwise.\n70\t//\n71\t// Example usage:\n72\t//\n73\t//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(response) {\n74\t//\t    // Handle 4xx client error (bad request, unauthorized, etc.)\n75\t//\t}\n76\tfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n77\t\tif resp == nil {\n78\t\t\treturn false\n79\t\t}\n80\t\n81\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500\n82\t}\n83\t\n84\t// HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates a server error (5xx).\n85\t//\n86\t// Returns true if the response status code is in the 500-599 range, false otherwise.\n87\t//\n88\t// Example usage:\n89\t//\n90\t//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError(response) {\n91\t//\t    // Handle 5xx server error (internal server error, bad gateway, etc.)\n92\t//\t}\n93\tfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n94\t\tif resp == nil {\n95\t\t\treturn false\n96\t\t}\n97\t\n98\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600\n99\t}\n100\t\n101\t// ContentTypeIsValid checks if a response's Content-Type header matches the expected pattern.\n102\t// It supports pattern matching where the base content-type matches regardless of parameters.\n103\t// For example, \"application/json\" will match \"application/json; charset=utf-8\".\n104\t//\n105\t// Parameters:\n106\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n107\t//   - expected: The expected content-type pattern (e.g., \"application/json\")\n108\t//\n109\t// Returns true if the response Content-Type matches the expected pattern, false otherwise.\n110\t// Returns false if the response is nil or has no Content-Type header.\n111\t//\n112\t// Example usage:\n113\t//\n114\t//\tif ContentTypeIsValid(response, \"application/json\") {\n115\t//\t    // Handle JSON response\n116\t//\t}\n117\tfunc ContentTypeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected string) bool {\n118\t\tif resp == nil {\n119\t\t\treturn false\n120\t\t}\n121\t\n122\t\tactual := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n123\t\tif actual == \"\" {\n124\t\t\treturn false\n125\t\t}\n126\t\n127\t\t// Parse both content-type strings to extract base media types\n128\t\tactualMediaType := parseContentType(actual)\n129\t\texpectedMediaType := parseContentType(expected)\n130\t\n131\t\treturn actualMediaType == expectedMediaType\n132\t}\n133\t\n134\t// parseContentType extracts the base media type from a content-type string.\n135\t// It strips parameters like charset, boundary, etc.\n136\t// For example, \"application/json; charset=utf-8\" becomes \"application/json\".\n137\tfunc parseContentType(contentType string) string {\n138\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n139\t\t\treturn \"\"\n140\t\t}\n141\t\n142\t\t// Split by semicolon to separate media type from parameters\n143\t\t// The first part before any semicolon is the base media type\n144\t\tfor i, c := range contentType {\n145\t\t\tif c == ';' {\n146\t\t\t\treturn trimSpace(contentType[:i])\n147\t\t\t}\n148\t\t}\n149\t\n150\t\t// No parameters found, return the whole string trimmed\n151\t\treturn trimSpace(contentType)\n152\t}\n153\t\n154\t// trimSpace removes leading and trailing whitespace from a string.\n155\tfunc trimSpace(s string) string {\n156\t\tstart := 0\n157\t\tend := len(s)\n158\t\n159\t\tfor start < end && (s[start] == ' ' || s[start] == '\\t' || s[start] == '\\n' || s[start] == '\\r') {\n160\t\t\tstart++\n161\t\t}\n162\t\n163\t\tfor end > start && (s[end-1] == ' ' || s[end-1] == '\\t' || s[end-1] == '\\n' || s[end-1] == '\\r') {\n164\t\t\tend--\n165\t\t}\n166\t\n167\t\treturn s[start:end]\n168\t}\n169\t\n170\t// ErrorResponseFieldNames specifies custom field names to check when validating error response structures.\n171\t// If nil, the default field names \"error\" and \"message\" are checked.\n172\ttype ErrorResponseFieldNames struct {\n173\t\t// PrimaryFieldName is the main error field to check (e.g., \"error\", \"detail\", \"error_description\")\n174\t\tPrimaryFieldName string\n175\t\t// SecondaryFieldName is an optional secondary field to check (e.g., \"message\", \"description\")\n176\t\tSecondaryFieldName string\n177\t}\n178\t\n179\t// DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames returns the default field names used for error response validation.\n180\t// This checks for both \"error\" and \"message\" fields.\n181\tfunc DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames() *ErrorResponseFieldNames {\n182\t\treturn &ErrorResponseFieldNames{\n183\t\t\tPrimaryFieldName:   \"error\",\n184\t\t\tSecondaryFieldName: \"message\",\n185\t\t}\n186\t}\n187\t\n188\t// ErrorResponseStructureIsValid validates whether a response object contains a valid error response structure.\n189\t// It checks for the presence of common error fields and returns whether the structure is valid.\n190\t//\n191\t// The response parameter can be:\n192\t//   - map[string]interface{}: A parsed JSON object representing the response body\n193\t//   - Any type that can be inspected for error fields using reflection\n194\t//\n195\t// The fieldNames parameter allows customization of which fields to check:\n196\t//   - If nil, checks for default fields \"error\" and \"message\"\n197\t//   - If provided, checks for the specified primary and secondary field names\n198\t//\n199\t// Returns true if the response contains at least one of the expected error fields with a non-empty value,\n200\t// false otherwise.\n201\t//\n202\t// Example usage:\n203\t//\n204\t//\t// Using default field names (checks for \"error\" and \"message\")\n205\t//\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\"error\": \"Invalid input\"}\n206\t//\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, nil)\n207\t//\n208\t//\t// Using custom field names\n209\t//\tcustomFields := &ErrorResponseFieldNames{PrimaryFieldName: \"detail\", SecondaryFieldName: \"description\"}\n210\t//\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\"detail\": \"Resource not found\"}\n211\t//\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, customFields)\n212\tfunc ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(response interface{}, fieldNames *ErrorResponseFieldNames) bool {\n213\t\tif response == nil {\n214\t\t\treturn false\n215\t\t}\n216\t\n217\t\t// Use default field names if not provided\n218\t\tif fieldNames == nil {\n219\t\t\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\t// Try to convert to map for field checking\n223\t\trespMap, ok := response.(map[string]interface{})\n224\t\tif !ok {\n225\t\t\treturn false\n226\t\t}\n227\t\n228\t\t// Check primary field\n229\t\tif fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName != \"\" {\n230\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.PrimaryFieldName]; exists {\n231\t\t\t\tif isNonEmptyValue(value) {\n232\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n233\t\t\t\t}\n234\t\t\t}\n235\t\t}\n236\t\n237\t\t// Check secondary field\n238\t\tif fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName != \"\" {\n239\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName]; exists {\n240\t\t\t\tif isNonEmptyValue(value) {\n241\t\t\t\t\treturn true\n242\t\t\t\t}\n243\t\t\t}\n244\t\t}\n245\t\n246\t\treturn false\n247\t}\n248\t\n249\t// isNonEmptyValue checks if a value is non-empty and meaningful.\n250\t// Returns true for strings with content, numbers (non-zero), and true booleans.\n251\t// Returns false for empty strings, zero values, false booleans, and nil.\n252\tfunc isNonEmptyValue(value interface{}) bool {\n253\t\tif value == nil {\n254\t\t\treturn false\n255\t\t}\n256\t\n257\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n258\t\tcase string:\n259\t\t\treturn v != \"\"\n260\t\tcase int:\n261\t\t\treturn v != 0\n262\t\tcase int8:\n263\t\t\treturn v != 0\n264\t\tcase int16:\n265\t\t\treturn v != 0\n266\t\tcase int32:\n267\t\t\treturn v != 0\n268\t\tcase int64:\n269\t\t\treturn v != 0\n270\t\tcase uint:\n271\t\t\treturn v != 0\n272\t\tcase uint8:\n273\t\t\treturn v != 0\n274\t\tcase uint16:\n275\t\t\treturn v != 0\n276\t\tcase uint32:\n277\t\t\treturn v != 0\n278\t\tcase uint64:\n279\t\t\treturn v != 0\n280\t\tcase float32:\n281\t\t\treturn v != 0.0\n282\t\tcase float64:\n283\t\t\treturn v != 0.0\n284\t\tcase bool:\n285\t\t\treturn v\n286\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n287\t\t\treturn len(v) > 0\n288\t\tcase []interface{}:\n289\t\t\treturn len(v) > 0\n290\t\tdefault:\n291\t\t\treturn true\n292\t\t}\n293\t}\n294\t\n295\t// CORSConfig specifies expected CORS header values for validation.\n296\ttype CORSConfig struct {\n297\t\t// AllowOrigin is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Origin.\n298\t\t// Use \"*\" for wildcard CORS, or specify an exact origin like \"https://example.com\".\n299\t\t// If empty, the origin header is not validated.\n300\t\tAllowOrigin string\n301\t\n302\t\t// AllowMethods is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Methods.\n303\t\t// If empty, the methods header is not validated.\n304\t\tAllowMethods string\n305\t\n306\t\t// AllowHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Headers.\n307\t\t// If empty, the headers header is not validated.\n308\t\tAllowHeaders string\n309\t\n310\t\t// AllowCredentials is the expected value for Access-Control-Allow-Credentials.\n311\t\t// If true, expects \"true\"; if false, expects \"true\" or header not present.\n312\t\t// This is typically used with specific origins (not wildcard).\n313\t\tAllowCredentials bool\n314\t\n315\t\t// ExposeHeaders is the expected value for Access-Control-Expose-Headers.\n316\t\t// If empty, the expose headers header is not validated.\n317\t\tExposeHeaders string\n318\t\n319\t\t// MaxAge is the expected value for Access-Control-Max-Age.\n320\t\t// If empty, the max-age header is not validated.\n321\t\tMaxAge string\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t// CORSHeadersIsValid validates CORS headers on an HTTP response, particularly for error responses.\n325\t// It checks the presence and values of common CORS headers and returns whether they match the expected configuration.\n326\t//\n327\t// This function is particularly useful for validating that error responses (4xx/5xx) include proper CORS headers,\n328\t// which is required for browsers to receive and process error responses from cross-origin requests.\n329\t//\n330\t// Parameters:\n331\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n332\t//   - config: Expected CORS configuration (use nil for basic validation that only checks header presence)\n333\t//\n334\t// Returns true if all specified CORS headers are present and match expected values, false otherwise.\n335\t// Returns false if the response is nil.\n336\t//\n337\t// When config is nil, only validates that Access-Control-Allow-Origin header exists (non-empty).\n338\t// When config is provided, validates only the fields that are non-empty in the config.\n339\t//\n340\t// Example usage:\n341\t//\n342\t//\t// Basic validation - check if CORS headers exist\n343\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, nil) {\n344\t//\t    // CORS headers are present\n345\t//\t}\n346\t//\n347\t//\t// Validate specific origin\n348\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\"}\n349\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n350\t//\t    // CORS headers match expected origin\n351\t//\t}\n352\t//\n353\t//\t// Validate wildcard CORS\n354\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{AllowOrigin: \"*\", AllowCredentials: false}\n355\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n356\t//\t    // Wildcard CORS is properly configured\n357\t//\t}\n358\t//\n359\t//\t// Validate full CORS configuration\n360\t//\tconfig := &CORSConfig{\n361\t//\t    AllowOrigin: \"https://example.com\",\n362\t//\t    AllowMethods: \"GET, POST, OPTIONS\",\n363\t//\t    AllowHeaders: \"Content-Type, Authorization\",\n364\t//\t    AllowCredentials: true,\n365\t//\t}\n366\t//\tif CORSHeadersIsValid(errorResponse, config) {\n367\t//\t    // Full CORS configuration is valid\n368\t//\t}\n369\tfunc CORSHeadersIsValid(resp *http.Response, config *CORSConfig) bool {\n370\t\tif resp == nil {\n371\t\t\treturn false\n372\t\t}\n373\t\n374\t\t// If no config provided, do basic validation: check if Access-Control-Allow-Origin exists\n375\t\tif config == nil {\n376\t\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n377\t\t\treturn origin != \"\"\n378\t\t}\n379\t\n380\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Origin if specified\n381\t\tif config.AllowOrigin != \"\" {\n382\t\t\torigin := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n383\t\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n384\t\t\t\treturn false\n385\t\t\t}\n386\t\n387\t\t\t// Check for wildcard or exact match\n388\t\t\tif config.AllowOrigin == \"*\" {\n389\t\t\t\t// Wildcard is valid\n390\t\t\t\tif origin != \"*\" {\n391\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n392\t\t\t\t}\n393\t\t\t} else {\n394\t\t\t\t// Exact origin match (case-sensitive for origins)\n395\t\t\t\tif origin != config.AllowOrigin {\n396\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n397\t\t\t\t}\n398\t\t\t}\n399\t\t}\n400\t\n401\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Methods if specified\n402\t\tif config.AllowMethods != \"\" {\n403\t\t\tmethods := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n404\t\t\tif methods != config.AllowMethods {\n405\t\t\t\treturn false\n406\t\t\t}\n407\t\t}\n408\t\n409\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Headers if specified\n410\t\tif config.AllowHeaders != \"\" {\n411\t\t\theaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n412\t\t\tif headers != config.AllowHeaders {\n413\t\t\t\treturn false\n414\t\t\t}\n415\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Allow-Credentials if specified\n418\t\tif config.AllowCredentials {\n419\t\t\tcredentials := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Credentials\")\n420\t\t\tif credentials != \"true\" {\n421\t\t\t\treturn false\n422\t\t\t}\n423\t\t}\n424\t\n425\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Expose-Headers if specified\n426\t\tif config.ExposeHeaders != \"\" {\n427\t\t\texposeHeaders := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Expose-Headers\")\n428\t\t\tif exposeHeaders != config.ExposeHeaders {\n429\t\t\t\treturn false\n430\t\t\t}\n431\t\t}\n432\t\n433\t\t// Validate Access-Control-Max-Age if specified\n434\t\tif config.MaxAge != \"\" {\n435\t\t\tmaxAge := resp.Header.Get(\"Access-Control-Max-Age\")\n436\t\t\tif maxAge != config.MaxAge {\n437\t\t\t\treturn false\n438\t\t\t}\n439\t\t}\n440\t\n441\t\treturn true\n442\t}\n443\t\n444\t// =============================================================================\n445\t// ERROR MESSAGE PATTERN VALIDATION\n446\t// =============================================================================\n447\t\n448\t// ErrorMessagePattern specifies pattern matching configuration for error messages.\n449\ttype ErrorMessagePattern struct {\n450\t\t// Pattern is the regex pattern to match against error messages\n451\t\tPattern string\n452\t\n453\t\t// CaseInsensitive indicates whether matching should be case-insensitive\n454\t\tCaseInsensitive bool\n455\t\n456\t\t// MatchAny indicates whether to match any field (true) or all fields (false)\n457\t\tMatchAny bool\n458\t\n459\t\t// FieldNames specifies which fields to check (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\")\n460\t\t// If empty, checks default fields: \"error\", \"message\"\n461\t\tFieldNames []string\n462\t}\n463\t\n464\t// ValidateErrorMessagePattern validates error message content against regex patterns.\n465\t//\n466\t// This function extracts error messages from response bodies and validates them\n467\t// against a regex pattern. It supports both exact matching and case-insensitive\n468\t// pattern matching for flexible error validation.\n469\t//\n470\t// Parameters:\n471\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n472\t//   - pattern: Regex pattern to match against error messages\n473\t//   - caseInsensitive: If true, performs case-insensitive matching\n474\t//\n475\t// Returns:\n476\t//   - true if the error message matches the pattern, false otherwise\n477\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails or response is invalid\n478\t//\n479\t// Example usage:\n480\t//\n481\t//\t// Check for \"not found\" error (case-insensitive)\n482\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `(?i)not found`, false)\n483\t//\n484\t//\t// Check for \"invalid.*token\" pattern\n485\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePattern(body, `invalid.*token`, true)\n486\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePattern(bodyBytes []byte, pattern string, caseInsensitive bool) (bool, error) {\n487\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n488\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n489\t\t}\n490\t\n491\t\tif pattern == \"\" {\n492\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n493\t\t}\n494\t\n495\t\t// Parse JSON body\n496\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n497\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n498\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n499\t\t}\n500\t\n501\t\t// Compile regex pattern\n502\t\tregexPattern := pattern\n503\t\tif caseInsensitive {\n504\t\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + pattern\n505\t\t}\n506\t\n507\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n508\t\tif err != nil {\n509\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n510\t\t}\n511\t\n512\t\t// Check default error fields\n513\t\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n514\t\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n515\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n516\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n517\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n518\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n519\t\t\t\t\t}\n520\t\t\t\t}\n521\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n522\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n523\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n524\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok {\n525\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n526\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n527\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n528\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n529\t\t\t\t\t}\n530\t\t\t\t}\n531\t\t\t}\n532\t\t}\n533\t\n534\t\treturn false, nil\n535\t}\n536\t\n537\t// ValidateErrorMessage validates error message content against a pattern.\n538\t//\n539\t// This function checks if a response body contains an error message that matches\n540\t// the expected pattern. It supports both regex patterns and simple substring matching.\n541\t// When the pattern is found, it returns nil. When the pattern is not found, it returns\n542\t// a descriptive error including a snippet of the actual response.\n543\t//\n544\t// The function automatically detects whether the pattern is a regex or substring:\n545\t// - If the pattern contains regex metacharacters (. * + ? ^ $ { } [ ] ( ) | \\), it's treated as regex\n546\t// - Otherwise, it's treated as a simple substring search (case-sensitive)\n547\t//\n548\t// Parameters:\n549\t//   - response: Raw response body bytes\n550\t//   - expectedPattern: Pattern to search for (regex or substring)\n551\t//\n552\t// Returns:\n553\t//   - nil if the pattern is found in the response\n554\t//   - error if the pattern is not found or if response is invalid\n555\t//\n556\t// Example usage:\n557\t//\n558\t//\t// Regex pattern matching\n559\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*token\")\n560\t//\tif err != nil {\n561\t//\t    // Pattern not found\n562\t//\t}\n563\t//\n564\t//\t// Simple substring matching\n565\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"not found\")\n566\t//\tif err != nil {\n567\t//\t    // Substring not found\n568\t//\t}\n569\t//\n570\t//\t// Common error patterns\n571\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"unauthorized\")        // OAuth errors\n572\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"invalid.*credentials\") // Auth errors\n573\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"rate.*limit\")          // Rate limiting\n574\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(body, \"timeout\")               // Timeout errors\n575\t//\n576\t// # Common Error Pattern Examples\n577\t//\n578\t// The function auto-detects regex vs substring matching:\n579\t//\n580\t// # OAuth 2.0 Errors\n581\t//\n582\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"error_description\": \"The access token expired\"}`)\n583\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid_token\")  // substring match\n584\t//\n585\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"access_denied\"}`)\n586\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"access_denied|invalid_grant\")  // regex match\n587\t//\n588\t// # Authentication Errors\n589\t//\n590\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Authentication failed: invalid credentials\"}`)\n591\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"authentication.*failed\")  // regex match\n592\t//\n593\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Invalid username or password\"}`)\n594\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*credentials\")  // regex match\n595\t//\n596\t// # Authorization Errors\n597\t//\n598\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Permission denied\"}`)\n599\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"permission.*denied\")  // regex match\n600\t//\n601\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"User is not authorized to access this resource\"}`)\n602\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not.*authorized\")  // regex match\n603\t//\n604\t// # Validation Errors\n605\t//\n606\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Validation failed: required field missing\"}`)\n607\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"validation.*failed\")  // regex match\n608\t//\n609\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"Invalid input: email format is incorrect\"}`)\n610\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"invalid.*input\")  // regex match\n611\t//\n612\t// # Resource Errors\n613\t//\n614\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Resource not found\"}`)\n615\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"not found\")  // substring match\n616\t//\n617\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"User does not exist\"}`)\n618\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"does not exist\")  // substring match\n619\t//\n620\t// # Rate Limiting Errors\n621\t//\n622\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Rate limit exceeded\"}`)\n623\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"rate.*limit\")  // regex match\n624\t//\n625\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"detail\": \"Too many requests, please try again later\"}`)\n626\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"too many requests\")  // substring match\n627\t//\n628\t// # Timeout Errors\n629\t//\n630\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Request timeout\"}`)\n631\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"timeout\")  // substring match\n632\t//\n633\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Connection timeout after 30 seconds\"}`)\n634\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"timeout\")  // substring match\n635\t//\n636\t// # Server Errors\n637\t//\n638\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"error\": \"Internal server error\"}`)\n639\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"internal.*error\")  // regex match\n640\t//\n641\t//\tresponse := []byte(`{\"message\": \"Service temporarily unavailable\"}`)\n642\t//\terr = ValidateErrorMessage(response, \"unavailable\")  // substring match\n643\tfunc ValidateErrorMessage(response []byte, expectedPattern string) error {\n644\t\t// Validate inputs\n645\t\tif len(response) == 0 {\n646\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n647\t\t}\n648\t\n649\t\tif expectedPattern == \"\" {\n650\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected pattern cannot be empty\")\n651\t\t}\n652\t\n653\t\t// Parse JSON body\n654\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n655\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(response, &body); err != nil {\n656\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n657\t\t}\n658\t\n659\t\t// Search for error messages in common fields\n660\t\tdefaultFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\", \"error_description\"}\n661\t\tvar foundMessages []string\n662\t\n663\t\tfor _, field := range defaultFields {\n664\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n665\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n666\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, strValue)\n667\t\t\t\t}\n668\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n669\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n670\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n671\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n672\t\t\t\t\t\t\tfoundMessages = append(foundMessages, strValue)\n673\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n674\t\t\t\t\t}\n675\t\t\t\t}\n676\t\t\t}\n677\t\t}\n678\t\n679\t\t// If no error messages found, return error with response snippet\n680\t\tif len(foundMessages) == 0 {\n681\t\t\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\n682\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no error message found in response body. Response snippet: %s\", snippet)\n683\t\t}\n684\t\n685\t\t// Detect if pattern is regex or substring\n686\t\tisRegex := containsRegexMetacharacters(expectedPattern)\n687\t\n688\t\t// Check each found message for pattern match\n689\t\tfor _, message := range foundMessages {\n690\t\t\tvar matched bool\n691\t\n692\t\t\tif isRegex {\n693\t\t\t\t// Try regex matching\n694\t\t\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(expectedPattern)\n695\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n696\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern '%s': %w\", expectedPattern, err)\n697\t\t\t\t}\n698\t\t\t\tmatched = re.MatchString(message)\n699\t\t\t} else {\n700\t\t\t\t// Simple substring matching (case-sensitive)\n701\t\t\t\tmatched = strings.Contains(message, expectedPattern)\n702\t\t\t}\n703\t\n704\t\t\tif matched {\n705\t\t\t\t// Pattern found - return nil (success)\n706\t\t\t\treturn nil\n707\t\t\t}\n708\t\t}\n709\t\n710\t\t// Pattern not found in any messages - return descriptive error\n711\t\tsnippet := extractResponseSnippet(response)\n712\t\tfirstMessage := foundMessages[0]\n713\t\tif len(foundMessages) > 1 {\n714\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"pattern '%s' not found in error messages. First message: \\\"%s\\\". Response snippet: %s\",\n715\t\t\t\texpectedPattern, firstMessage, snippet)\n716\t\t}\n717\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"pattern '%s' not found in error message: \\\"%s\\\". Response snippet: %s\",\n718\t\t\texpectedPattern, firstMessage, snippet)\n719\t}\n720\t\n721\t// containsRegexMetacharacters checks if a string contains regex metacharacters.\n722\t// This is used to auto-detect whether a pattern should be treated as regex or substring.\n723\tfunc containsRegexMetacharacters(pattern string) bool {\n724\t\tregexChars := []string{\".\", \"*\", \"+\", \"?\", \"^\", \"$\", \"{\", \"}\", \"[\", \"]\", \"(\", \")\", \"|\", \"\\\\\"}\n725\t\tfor _, char := range regexChars {\n726\t\t\tif strings.Contains(pattern, char) {\n727\t\t\t\treturn true\n728\t\t\t}\n729\t\t}\n730\t\treturn false\n731\t}\n732\t\n733\t// extractResponseSnippet creates a truncated snippet of the response body for error messages.\n734\t// It limits the snippet to 200 characters to keep error messages readable.\n735\tfunc extractResponseSnippet(response []byte) string {\n736\t\t// Convert to string and truncate\n737\t\tresponseStr := string(response)\n738\t\tmaxLen := 200\n739\t\tif len(responseStr) > maxLen {\n740\t\t\tresponseStr = responseStr[:maxLen] + \"...\"\n741\t\t}\n742\t\t// Replace newlines with spaces for single-line error message\n743\t\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \"\\n\", \" \")\n744\t\tresponseStr = strings.ReplaceAll(responseStr, \"\\r\", \" \")\n745\t\treturn responseStr\n746\t}\n747\t\n748\t// ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig validates error messages using advanced pattern configuration.\n749\t//\n750\t// This function provides flexible error message validation with support for:\n751\t// - Custom field selection\n752\t// - Case-insensitive matching\n753\t// - Match-any vs match-all semantics\n754\t// - Multiple pattern checking\n755\t//\n756\t// Parameters:\n757\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n758\t//   - config: Pattern matching configuration\n759\t//\n760\t// Returns:\n761\t//   - true if validation succeeds, false otherwise\n762\t//   - error if configuration is invalid or JSON parsing fails\n763\t//\n764\t// Example usage:\n765\t//\n766\t//\tconfig := &ErrorMessagePattern{\n767\t//\t    Pattern:         \"unauthorized\",\n768\t//\t    CaseInsensitive: true,\n769\t//\t    FieldNames:      []string{\"error\", \"detail\"},\n770\t//\t}\n771\t//\tmatches, err := ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(body, config)\n772\tfunc ValidateErrorMessagePatternWithConfig(bodyBytes []byte, config *ErrorMessagePattern) (bool, error) {\n773\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n774\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n775\t\t}\n776\t\n777\t\tif config == nil {\n778\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"config cannot be nil\")\n779\t\t}\n780\t\n781\t\tif config.Pattern == \"\" {\n782\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"pattern cannot be empty\")\n783\t\t}\n784\t\n785\t\t// Parse JSON body\n786\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n787\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n788\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n789\t\t}\n790\t\n791\t\t// Compile regex pattern\n792\t\tregexPattern := config.Pattern\n793\t\tif config.CaseInsensitive {\n794\t\t\tregexPattern = \"(?i)\" + config.Pattern\n795\t\t}\n796\t\n797\t\tre, err := regexp.Compile(regexPattern)\n798\t\tif err != nil {\n799\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid regex pattern: %w\", err)\n800\t\t}\n801\t\n802\t\t// Determine which fields to check\n803\t\tfieldsToCheck := config.FieldNames\n804\t\tif len(fieldsToCheck) == 0 {\n805\t\t\tfieldsToCheck = []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\"}\n806\t\t}\n807\t\n808\t\t// Check each specified field\n809\t\tmatchedFields := []string{}\n810\t\tfor _, field := range fieldsToCheck {\n811\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n812\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n813\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n814\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\n815\t\t\t\t\t\tif config.MatchAny {\n816\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n817\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n818\t\t\t\t\t}\n819\t\t\t\t}\n820\t\t\t\t// Check nested error objects\n821\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n822\t\t\t\t\tif nestedValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n823\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := nestedValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n824\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif re.MatchString(strValue) {\n825\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedFields = append(matchedFields, field)\n826\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif config.MatchAny {\n827\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n828\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n829\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n830\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n831\t\t\t\t\t}\n832\t\t\t\t}\n833\t\t\t}\n834\t\t}\n835\t\n836\t\t// For match-all semantics, require all specified fields to match\n837\t\tif !config.MatchAny {\n838\t\t\treturn len(matchedFields) == len(fieldsToCheck), nil\n839\t\t}\n840\t\n841\t\treturn false, nil\n842\t}\n843\t\n844\t// =============================================================================\n845\t// SPECIFIC ERROR CODE VALIDATION\n846\t// =============================================================================\n847\t\n848\t// ErrorCodeInResponse checks if a response body contains a specific error code.\n849\t//\n850\t// This function searches for error codes in common error response fields.\n851\t// It supports both simple string error codes and nested error objects.\n852\t//\n853\t// Parameters:\n854\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n855\t//   - expectedCode: The error code to look for (e.g., \"INVALID_TOKEN\", \"AUTH_FAILED\")\n856\t//\n857\t// Returns:\n858\t//   - true if the error code is found in the response, false otherwise\n859\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n860\t//\n861\t// Example usage:\n862\t//\n863\t//\t// Check for \"AUTH_FAILED\" error code\n864\t//\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"AUTH_FAILED\")\n865\t//\n866\t//\t// Check for OAuth \"invalid_token\" error\n867\t//\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"invalid_token\")\n868\tfunc ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes []byte, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\n869\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n870\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n871\t\t}\n872\t\n873\t\tif expectedCode == \"\" {\n874\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"expected code cannot be empty\")\n875\t\t}\n876\t\n877\t\t// Parse JSON body\n878\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n879\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n880\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n881\t\t}\n882\t\n883\t\t// Common error code field names\n884\t\terrorCodeFields := []string{\"error_code\", \"errorCode\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"}\n885\t\n886\t\t// Check for error code in standard fields\n887\t\tfor _, field := range errorCodeFields {\n888\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n889\t\t\t\t// Check for string match\n890\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok {\n891\t\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n892\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n893\t\t\t\t\t}\n894\t\t\t\t}\n895\t\t\t\t// Check for nested error code in error object\n896\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n897\t\t\t\t\tif codeValue, exists := nestedObj[\"code\"]; exists {\n898\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := codeValue.(string); ok {\n899\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n900\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n901\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n902\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n903\t\t\t\t\t}\n904\t\t\t\t}\n905\t\t\t}\n906\t\t}\n907\t\n908\t\t// Check for OAuth-style error field (contains error code directly)\n909\t\tif errorCode, exists := body[\"error\"]; exists {\n910\t\t\tif strValue, ok := errorCode.(string); ok {\n911\t\t\t\tif strings.EqualFold(strValue, expectedCode) {\n912\t\t\t\t\treturn true, nil\n913\t\t\t\t}\n914\t\t\t}\n915\t\t}\n916\t\n917\t\treturn false, nil\n918\t}\n919\t\n920\t// ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode validates both status code and error code in a single check.\n921\t//\n922\t// This is a convenience function that validates HTTP status code and error code\n923\t// together, ensuring that both the HTTP layer and application layer error\n924\t// information are consistent.\n925\t//\n926\t// Parameters:\n927\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n928\t//   - expectedStatus: Expected HTTP status code\n929\t//   - expectedCode: Expected error code in response body\n930\t//\n931\t// Returns:\n932\t//   - true if both status code and error code match, false otherwise\n933\t//   - error if body parsing fails\n934\t//\n935\t// Example usage:\n936\t//\n937\t//\t// Validate 401 response with \"UNAUTHORIZED\" error code\n938\t//\tvalid, err := ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp, 401, \"UNAUTHORIZED\")\n939\t//\n940\t//\t// Validate 403 response with \"FORBIDDEN\" error code\n941\t//\tvalid, err := ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp, 403, \"FORBIDDEN\")\n942\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeAndErrorCode(resp *http.Response, expectedStatus int, expectedCode string) (bool, error) {\n943\t\tif resp == nil {\n944\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"response is nil\")\n945\t\t}\n946\t\n947\t\t// Check status code\n948\t\tif resp.StatusCode != expectedStatus {\n949\t\t\treturn false, nil\n950\t\t}\n951\t\n952\t\t// Read response body\n953\t\tvar bodyBytes []byte\n954\t\tif resp.Body != nil {\n955\t\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n956\t\t\tvar err error\n957\t\t\tbodyBytes, err = io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n958\t\t\tif err != nil {\n959\t\t\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read response body: %w\", err)\n960\t\t\t}\n961\t\t}\n962\t\n963\t\t// Check error code\n964\t\tfound, err := ErrorCodeInResponse(bodyBytes, expectedCode)\n965\t\tif err != nil {\n966\t\t\treturn false, err\n967\t\t}\n968\t\n969\t\treturn found, nil\n970\t}\n971\t\n972\t// GetErrorMessage extracts the error message from a response body.\n973\t//\n974\t// This helper function searches for error messages in common fields and returns\n975\t// the first non-empty message found.\n976\t//\n977\t// Parameters:\n978\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n979\t//\n980\t// Returns:\n981\t//   - The extracted error message, or empty string if not found\n982\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n983\t//\n984\t// Example usage:\n985\t//\n986\t//\tmessage, err := GetErrorMessage(body)\n987\t//\tif message != \"\" {\n988\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Error: %s\\n\", message)\n989\t//\t}\n990\tfunc GetErrorMessage(bodyBytes []byte) (string, error) {\n991\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n992\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n993\t\t}\n994\t\n995\t\t// Parse JSON body\n996\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n997\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n998\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n999\t\t}\n1000\t\n1001\t\t// Check common message fields\n1002\t\tmessageFields := []string{\"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"description\", \"error_description\"}\n1003\t\tfor _, field := range messageFields {\n1004\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n1005\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1006\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1007\t\t\t\t}\n1008\t\t\t\t// Check nested message in error object\n1009\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n1010\t\t\t\t\tif messageValue, exists := nestedObj[\"message\"]; exists {\n1011\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := messageValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1012\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1013\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1014\t\t\t\t\t}\n1015\t\t\t\t}\n1016\t\t\t}\n1017\t\t}\n1018\t\n1019\t\treturn \"\", nil\n1020\t}\n1021\t\n1022\t// GetErrorCode extracts the error code from a response body.\n1023\t//\n1024\t// This helper function searches for error codes in common fields and returns\n1025\t// the first non-empty code found.\n1026\t//\n1027\t// Parameters:\n1028\t//   - bodyBytes: Raw response body bytes\n1029\t//\n1030\t// Returns:\n1031\t//   - The extracted error code, or empty string if not found\n1032\t//   - error if JSON parsing fails\n1033\t//\n1034\t// Example usage:\n1035\t//\n1036\t//\tcode, err := GetErrorCode(body)\n1037\t//\tif code != \"\" {\n1038\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Error code: %s\\n\", code)\n1039\t//\t}\n1040\tfunc GetErrorCode(bodyBytes []byte) (string, error) {\n1041\t\tif len(bodyBytes) == 0 {\n1042\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"response body is empty\")\n1043\t\t}\n1044\t\n1045\t\t// Parse JSON body\n1046\t\tvar body map[string]interface{}\n1047\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &body); err != nil {\n1048\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse response body: %w\", err)\n1049\t\t}\n1050\t\n1051\t\t// Check common code fields\n1052\t\tcodeFields := []string{\"error_code\", \"errorCode\", \"code\", \"error\"}\n1053\t\tfor _, field := range codeFields {\n1054\t\t\tif value, exists := body[field]; exists {\n1055\t\t\t\t// Direct string code\n1056\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := value.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1057\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1058\t\t\t\t}\n1059\t\t\t\t// Check nested code in error object\n1060\t\t\t\tif nestedObj, ok := value.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n1061\t\t\t\t\tif codeValue, exists := nestedObj[\"code\"]; exists {\n1062\t\t\t\t\t\tif strValue, ok := codeValue.(string); ok && strValue != \"\" {\n1063\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn strValue, nil\n1064\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1065\t\t\t\t\t}\n1066\t\t\t\t}\n1067\t\t\t}\n1068\t\t}\n1069\t\n1070\t\treturn \"\", nil\n1071\t}\n1072\t\n1073\t// =============================================================================\n1074\t// DETAILED VALIDATION RESULTS\n1075\t// =============================================================================\n1076\t\n1077\t// StatusCodeValidationResult provides detailed information about status code validation.\n1078\t// It includes not just whether validation passed, but also the expected vs actual values\n1079\t// and detailed mismatch information to help diagnose validation failures.\n1080\ttype StatusCodeValidationResult struct {\n1081\t\t// Valid indicates whether the validation passed\n1082\t\tValid bool\n1083\t\t// ActualCode is the HTTP status code from the response\n1084\t\tActualCode int\n1085\t\t// ExpectedCodes contains the expected status code(s) that were validated against\n1086\t\tExpectedCodes []int\n1087\t\t// MatchedCode is the specific expected code that matched (if any)\n1088\t\tMatchedCode *int\n1089\t\t// MismatchDetails contains human-readable information about why validation failed\n1090\t\tMismatchDetails string\n1091\t\t// IsClientError indicates whether the actual code is a client error (4xx)\n1092\t\tIsClientError bool\n1093\t\t// IsServerError indicates whether the actual code is a server error (5xx)\n1094\t\tIsServerError bool\n1095\t\t// Category describes the general category of the actual status code\n1096\t\tCategory string\n1097\t}\n1098\t\n1099\t// ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails performs comprehensive status code validation with detailed results.\n1100\t// Unlike HTTPStatusCodeIsValid which returns only a boolean, this function provides detailed information\n1101\t// about the validation process, making it easier to diagnose why validation failed.\n1102\t//\n1103\t// Parameters:\n1104\t//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n1105\t//   - expected: Expected status code(s) - can be a single int or []int\n1106\t//\n1107\t// Returns a StatusCodeValidationResult with detailed validation information.\n1108\t//\n1109\t// Example usage:\n1110\t//\n1111\t//\tresult := ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(response, []int{200, 201, 204})\n1112\t//\tif !result.Valid {\n1113\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status validation failed: %s\", result.MismatchDetails)\n1114\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Got: %d, expected one of: %v\", result.ActualCode, log.Printf(\"Status code is valid: %d\", result.ActualCode)\n1121\t//\t}\n1122\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeWithDetails(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}) StatusCodeValidationResult {\n1123\t\tresult := StatusCodeValidationResult{\n1124\t\t\tValid:             false,\n1125\t\t\tActualCode:        0,\n1126\t\t\tExpectedCodes:     []int{},\n1127\t\t\tMatchedCode:       nil,\n1128\t\t\tMismatchDetails:   \"\",\n1129\t\t\tIsClientError:     false,\n1130\t\t\tIsServerError:     false,\n1131\t\t\tCategory:          \"unknown\",\n1132\t\t}\n1133\t\n1134\t\tif resp == nil result\n1189\t}\n1190\t\n1191\t// StatusCodeRange defines a range of status codes for flexible validation.\n1192\t// This is useful when you want to accept any status code within a certain range.\n1193\ttype StatusCodeRange struct {\n1194\t\t// Min is the minimum status code in the range (inclusive)\n1195\t\tMin int\n1196\t\t// Max is the maximum status code in the range (inclusive)\n1197\t\tMax int\n1198\t\t// Description is a human-readable description of what this range represents\n1199\t\tDescription string\n1200\t}\n1201\t\n1202\t// Common status code ranges\n1203\tvar (\n1204\t\t// Range1xx covers informational responses (100-199)\n1205\t\tRange1xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 100, Max: 199, Description: \"Informational\"}\n1206\t\t// Range2xx covers successful responses (200-299)\n1207\t\tRange2xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 200, Max: 299, Description: \"Success\"}\n1208\t\t// Range3xx covers redirection responses (300-399)\n1209\t\tRange3xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 300, Max: 399, Description: \"Redirection\"}\n1210\t\t// Range4xx covers client error responses (400-499)\n1211\t\tRange4xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 400, Max: 499, Description: \"Client Error\"}\n1212\t\t// Range5xx covers server error responses (500-599)\n1213\t\tRange5xx = StatusCodeRange{Min: 500, Max: 599, Description: \"Server Error\"}\n1214\t)\n1215\t\n1216\t// ValidateStatusCodeRange checks if a response's status code falls within a specified range.\n1217\t// This is useful for flexible validation where you want to accept any status code within a {\n1244\t\tif resp == nil {\n1245\t\t\treturn false\n1246\t\t}\n1247\t\n1248\t\tif statusRange.Min > statusRange.Max {\n1249\t\t\treturn false\n1250\t\t}\n1251\t\n1252\t\treturn resp.StatusCode >= (should all be false)\n1352\t\tMustNotContainResults map[string]bool\n1353\t\t// LengthValidation indicates whether the message length was valid\n1354\t\tLengthValidation bool\n1355\t\t// Issues ValidateErrorMessageWithDetails(responseBody interface{}, pattern EnhancedErrorMessagePattern) ErrorMessageValidationResult {\n1382\t\tresult := ErrorMessageValidationResult{\n1383\t\t\tValid:                 false,\n1384\t\t\tFound:                 false,\n1385\t\t\tMessage:               \"\",\n1386\t\t\tFieldName:             \"\",\n1387\t\t\tPatternMatched:        false,\n1388\t\t\tMustContainResults:    make(map[string]bool),\n1389\t\t\tMustNotContainResults: make(map[string]bool),\n1390\t\t\tLengthValidation:      true,\n1391\t\t\tIssues:                []string{},\n1392\t\t}\n1393\t\n1394\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n1395\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"response body is nil\")\n1396\t\t\treturn result\n1397\t\t}\n1398\t\n1399\t\t// Use default field names if not provided\n1400\t\tfieldNames := pattern.FieldNames\n1401\t\tif fieldNames == nil {\n1402\t\t\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\n1403\t\t}\n1404\t\n1405\t\t// fieldNames.SecondaryFieldName\n1432\t\t\t\t}\n1433\t\t\t}\n1434\t\t}\n1435\t\n1436\t\tresult.Found = foundMessage != \"\"\n1437\t\tresult.Message = foundMessage\n1438\t\tresult.FieldName = foundFieldName\n1439\t\n1440\t\tif !result.Found {\n1441\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, \"no valid error message field found\")\n1442\t\t\treturn result\n1443\t\t}\n1444\t\n1445\t\t// Validate length\n1446\t\tif pattern.MinLength > 0 && len(foundMessage) < pattern.MinLength {\n1447\t\t\tresult.LengthValidation = false\n1448\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message length %d is less than minimum %d\", len(foundMessage), pattern.MinLength))\n1449\t\t}\n1450\t\n1451\t\tif pattern.MaxLength > 0 && len(foundMessage) > pattern.MaxLength {\n1452\t\t\tresult.LengthValidation = false\n1453\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message length %d exceeds maximum %d\", len(foundMessage), pattern.MaxLength))\n1454\t\t}\n1455\t\n1456\t\t// Validate regex pattern\n1457\t\tif pattern.Pattern != \"\" {\n1458\t\t\tregex := regexp.MustCompile(pattern.Pattern)\n1459\t\t\tif !result.PatternMatched {\n1464\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message does not match required pattern: %s\", pattern.Pattern))\n1465\t\t\t}\n1466\t\t} else {\n1467\t\t\tresult.PatternMatched = true // No pattern specified, consider it matched\n1468\t\t}\n1469\t\n1470\t\t// Validate MustContain strings\n1471\t\tallMustContainFound := true\n1472\t\tfor _, mustContain := range pattern.MustContain {\n1473\t\t\tvar found bool\n1474\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1475\t\t\t\t// Case-insensitive search\n1476\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustContain))\n1477\t\t\t} else {\n1478\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustContain)\n1479\t\t\t}\n1480\t\t\tresult.MustContainResults[mustContain] = found\n1481\t\t\tif !found {\n1482\t\t\t\tallMustContainFound = false\n1483\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message does not contain required string: %s\", mustContain))\n1484\t\t\t}\n1485\t\t}\n1486\t\n1487\t\t// Validate MustNotContain strings\n1488\t\thasForbiddenStrings := false\n1489\t\tfor _, mustNotContain := range pattern.MustNotContain {\n1490\t\t\tvar found bool\n1491\t\t\tif pattern.CaseInsensitive {\n1492\t\t\t\t// Case-insensitive search\n1493\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(foundMessage), strings.ToLower(mustNotContain))\n1494\t\t\t} else {\n1495\t\t\t\tfound = strings.Contains(foundMessage, mustNotContain)\n1496\t\t\t}\n1497\t\t\tresult.MustNotContainResults[mustNotContain] = found\n1498\t\t\tif found {\n1499\t\t\t\thasForbiddenStrings = true\n1500\t\t\t\tresult.Issues = append(result.Issues, fmt.Sprintf(\"message contains forbidden string: %s\", mustNotContain))\n1501\t\t\t}\n1502\t\t}\n1503\t\n1504\t\t// Overall validity\n1505\t\tresult.Valid = result.Found && result.LengthValidation && result.PatternMatched &&\n1506\t\t\tallMustContainFound && !hasForbiddenStrings\n1507\t\n1508\t\treturn result\n1509\t}\n1510\t\n1511\t// =============================================================================\n1512\t// ERROR CODE DETECTION\n1513\t// =============================================================================\n1514\t\n1515\t// ErrorCodeMatch represents a found error code in a response.\n1516\ttype ErrorCodeMatch struct \"status\"}\n1583\t\t\t}\n1584\t\n1585\t\t\tfor _, fieldName := range fieldsToCheck {\n1586\t\t\t\tif value, exists := respMap[fieldName]; exists {\n1587\t\t\t\t\tvar codeValue string:\n1594\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = v\n1595\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"string\"\n1596\t\t\t\t\t\t// Try to parse as number\n1597\t\t\t\t\t\tif num, err := parseIntFromString(v); err == nil && &v\n1603\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"numeric\"\n1604\t\t\t\t\tcase float64:\n1605\t\t\t\t\t\t// JSON numbers are often parsed as float64\n1606\t\t\t\t\t\tif v == float64(int(v)) {\n1607\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnum := int(v)\n1608\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", num)\n1609\t\t\t\t\t\t\tnumericCode = &num\n1610\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"numeric\"\n1611\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1612\t\t\t\t\tcase bool:\n1613\t\t\t\t\t\t// Some APIs use true/false as error codes\n1614\t\t\t\t\t\tif v {\n1615\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = \"true\"\n1616\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"boolean\"\n1617\t\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n1618\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcodeValue = \"false\"\n1619\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = \"boolean\"\n1620\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1621\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n1622\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue // Skip unsupported types\n1623\t\t\t\t\t}\n1624\t\n1625\t\t\t\t\t// Validate against value patterns if specified\n1626\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched := true\n1627\t\t\t\t\tif len(pattern.ValuePatterns) > 0 {\n1628\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = false\n1629\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, valuePattern := range pattern.ValuePatterns {\n1630\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatched, _ := regexp.MatchString(valuePattern, codeValue)\n1631\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif matched {\n1632\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = true\n1633\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tmatchedPattern = valuePattern\n1634\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n1635\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1636\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n1637\t\t\t\t\t}\n1638\t\n1639\t\t\t\t\t// Check numeric-only constraint\n1640\t\t\t\t\tif pattern.NumericOnly && numericCode == nil {\n1641\t\t\t\t\t\tpatternsMatched = false\n1642\t\t\t\t\t}\n1643\t\n1644\t\t\t\t\tif patternsMatched && codeValue != \"\" {\n1645\t\t\t\t\t\tmatches = append(matches, ErrorCodeMatch{\n1646\t\t\t\t\t\t\tFieldName:      fieldName,\n1647\t\t\t\t\t\t\tCodeValue:      codeValue,\n1648\t\t\t\t\t\t\tNumericCode:    numericCode,\n1649\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMatchedPattern: matchedPattern,\n1650\t\t\t\t\t\t\tPosition:       fmt.Sprintf(\"field '%s' in response body\", fieldName),\n1651\t\t\t\t\t\t})\n1652\t\t\t\t\t}\n1653\t\t\t\t}\n1654\t\t\t}\n1655\t\t}\n1656\t\n1657\t\treturn matches\n1658\t}\n1659\t\n1660\t// ValidateErrorCodeInResponse checks if a specific error code is present in a response.\n1661\t// This is a convenience wrapper around FindErrorCodesInResponse for checking a single code.\n1662\t//\n1663\t// Parameters:\n1664\t//   - responseBody: The parsed response body\n1665\t//   - expectedCode: The error code value to look for (as string)\n1666\t//   - fieldName: The field name to check (empty string to check all common fields)\n1667\t//\n1668\t// Returns true if the expected error code is found, false otherwise.\n1669\t//\n1670\t// Example usage:\n1671\t//\n1672\t//\tbody, _ := parseJSON(response.Body)\n1673\t//\thasInvalidToken := ValidateErrorCodeInResponse(body, \"invalid_token\", \"error\")\n1674\t//\tif hasInvalidToken {\n1675\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Response contains invalid_token error\")\n1676\t//\t}\n1677\tfunc ValidateErrorCodeInResponse(responseBody interface{}, expectedCode string, fieldName string) bool {\n1678\t\tif responseBody == nil {\n1679\t\t\treturn false\n1680\t\t}\n1681\t\n1682\t\tvar pattern EnhancedErrorCodePattern\n1683\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1684\t\t\tpattern = EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n1685\t\t\t\tFieldNames: []string{fieldName},\n1686\t\t\t}\n1687\t\t} else {\n1688\t\t\tpattern = EnhancedErrorCodePattern{\n1689\t\t\t\tFieldNames: []string{\"error_code\", \"code\", \"error\", \"status\"},\n1690\t\t\t}\n1691\t\t}\n1692\t\n1693\t\tmatches := FindErrorCodesInResponse(responseBody, []EnhancedErrorCodePattern{pattern})\n1694\t\tfor _, match := range matches {\n1695\t\t\tif match.CodeValue == expectedCode {\n1696\t\t\t\treturn true\n1697\t\t\t}\n1698\t\t}\n1699\t\n1700\t\treturn false\n1701\t}\n1702\t\n1703\t// parseIntFromString attempts to parse a string as an integer.\n1704\tfunc parseIntFromString(s string) (int, error) {\n1705\t\tvar result int\n1706\t\t_, err := fmt.Sscanf(s, \"%d\", &result)\n1707\t\treturn result, err\n1708\t}\n1709\t\n1710\t// =============================================================================\n1711\t// STATUS CODE RANGE VALIDATION (PATTERN-BASED)\n1712\t// =============================================================================\n1713\t\n1714\t// ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt validates a status code against a pattern string.\n1715\t// It supports patterns like '4xx', '5xx', '3xx', '2xx', '1xx' to check if a status code\n1716\t// falls within a specified range.\n1717\t//\n1718\t// Parameters:\n1719\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\n1720\t//   - actual: The actual status code to validate\n1721\t//\n1722\t// Returns error if the pattern is invalid or if the actual code doesn't match the range.\n1723\t// Returns nil if the actual code falls within the specified range.\n1724\t//\n1725\t// Example usage:\n1726\t//\n1727\t//\t// Validate 404 is in 4xx range\n1728\t//\terr := ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\"4xx\", 404)\n1729\t//\tif err != nil {\n1730\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Status validation failed: %v\", err)\n1731\t//\t}\n1732\t//\n1733\t//\t// Validate 500 is in 5xx range\n1734\t//\terr = ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\"5xx\", 500)\n1735\t//\n1736\t//\t// This returns an error because 200 is not in 4xx range\n1737\t//\terr = ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(\"4xx\", 200)\n1738\tfunc ValidateStatusCodeRangeInt(pattern string, actual int) error {\n1739\t\t// Validate pattern format\n1740\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n1741\t\t\treturn FormatValidationError(\n1742\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n1743\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"pattern in format 'Nxx' (3 chars)\"),\n1744\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"'%s' (%d chars)\", pattern, len(pattern)),\n1745\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid pattern format: %s\", pattern),\n1746\t\t\t\t\"\",\n1747\t\t\t)\n1748\t\t}\n1749\t\n1750\t\t// Extract the century digit (first character)\n1751\t\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\n1752\t\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\n1753\t\t\treturn FormatValidationError(\n1754\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n1755\t\t\t\t\"century digit 1-5\",\n1756\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"'%c' (ASCII %d)\", centuryChar, centuryChar),\n1757\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid pattern century in '%s'\", pattern),\n1758\t\t\t\t\"\",\n1759\t\t\t)\n1760\t\t}\n1761\t\n1762\t\t// Validate 'xx' suffix\n1763\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n1764\t\t\treturn FormatValidationError(\n1765\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n1766\t\t\t\t\"pattern ending with 'xx'\",\n1767\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", pattern[1:]),\n1768\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"invalid pattern suffix in '%s'\", pattern),\n1769\t\t\t\t\"\",\n1770\t\t\t)\n1771\t\t}\n1772\t\n1773\t\t// Calculate the range from the pattern\n1774\t\tcentury := int(centuryChar - '0')\n1775\t\tminRange := century * 100\n1776\t\tmaxRange := minRange + 99\n1777\t\n1778\t\t// Get description for the range\n1779\t\tdesc, _ := GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n1780\t\n1781\t\t// Check if actual code is within the range\n1782\t\tif actual < minRange || actual > maxRange {\n1783\t\t\texpectedRange := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d-%d)\", pattern, minRange, maxRange)\n1784\t\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n1785\t\t\t\texpectedRange = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s (%d-%d)\", pattern, desc, minRange, maxRange)\n1786\t\t\t}\n1787\t\n1788\t\t\treturn FormatValidationError(\n1789\t\t\t\t\"status_code_range\",\n1790\t\t\t\texpectedRange,\n1791\t\t\t\tactual,\n1792\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"status code validation failed for pattern '%s'\", pattern),\n1793\t\t\t\t\"\",\n1794\t\t\t)\n1795\t\t}\n1796\t\n1797\t\treturn nil\n1798\t}\n1799\t\n1800\t// ParseStatusCodeRange extracts the century digit and range from a pattern string.\n1801\t// This is a helper function for advanced use cases where you need the range bounds.\n1802\t//\n1803\t// Parameters:\n1804\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\n1805\t//\n1806\t// Returns the min and max values of the range, or an error if the pattern is invalid.\n1807\t//\n1808\t// Example usage:\n1809\t//\n1810\t//\tmin, max, err := ParseStatusCodeRange(\"4xx\")\n1811\t//\tif err != nil {\n1812\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Invalid pattern: %v\", err)\n1813\t//\t} else {\n1814\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Range: %d-%d\\n\", min, max)\n1815\t//\t}\n1816\tfunc ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern string) (min int, max int, err error) {\n1817\t\t// Validate pattern format\n1818\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n1819\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern format: %s (expected format: '4xx', '5xx', etc.)\", pattern)\n1820\t\t}\n1821\t\n1822\t\t// Extract the century digit (first character)\n1823\t\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\n1824\t\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\n1825\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern century: %c (must be 1-5)\", centuryChar)\n1826\t\t}\n1827\t\n1828\t\t// Validate 'xx' suffix\n1829\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n1830\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern suffix: %s (expected 'xx')\", pattern)\n1831\t\t}\n1832\t\n1833\t\t// Calculate the range from the pattern\n1834\t\tcentury := int(centuryChar - '0')\n1835\t\tminRange := century * 100\n1836\t\tmaxRange := minRange + 99\n1837\t\n1838\t\treturn minRange, maxRange, nil\n1839\t}\n1840\t\n1841\t// GetStatusCodeRangeDescription returns a human-readable description for a status code range pattern.\n1842\t//\n1843\t// Parameters:\n1844\t//   - pattern: The pattern string (e.g., '4xx', '5xx', '2xx')\n1845\t//\n1846\t// Returns a description string or an error if the pattern is invalid.\n1847\t//\n1848\t// Example usage:\n1849\t//\n1850\t//\tdesc, err := GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(\"4xx\")\n1851\t//\tif err != nil {\n1852\t//\t    log.Printf(\"Invalid pattern: %v\", err)\n1853\t//\t} else {\n1854\t//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Description: %s\\n\", desc)\n1855\t//\t}\n1856\tfunc GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern string) (string, error) {\n1857\t\tif len(pattern) != 3 {\n1858\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern format: %s\", pattern)\n1859\t\t}\n1860\t\n1861\t\tcenturyChar := pattern[0]\n1862\t\tif centuryChar < '1' || centuryChar > '5' {\n1863\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern century: %c\", centuryChar)\n1864\t\t}\n1865\t\n1866\t\tif pattern[1] != 'x' || pattern[2] != 'x' {\n1867\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid pattern suffix: %s\", pattern)\n1868\t\t}\n1869\t\n1870\t\tdescriptions := map[byte]string{\n1871\t\t\t'1': \"Informational (1xx)\",\n1872\t\t\t'2': \"Success (2xx)\",\n1873\t\t\t'3': \"Redirection (3xx)\",\n1874\t\t\t'4': \"Client Error (4xx)\",\n1875\t\t\t'5': \"Server Error (5xx)\",\n1876\t\t}\n1877\t\n1878\t\tdesc, ok := descriptions[centuryChar]\n1879\t\tif !ok {\n1880\t\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"unknown century: %c\", centuryChar)\n1881\t\t}\n1882\t\n1883\t\treturn desc, nil\n1884\t}\n1885\t\n1886\t// =============================================================================\n1887\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS FOR CREATING VALIDATION ERRORS\n1888\t// =============================================================================\n1889\t\n1890\t// FormatValidationError creates a standardized ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n1891\t//\n1892\t// This helper function automatically generates relevant suggestions based on the\n1893\t// validation type and the mismatch between expected and actual values.\n1894\t//\n1895\t// Parameters:\n1896\t//   - validationType: The type of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\")\n1897\t//   - expected: The expected value\n1898\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n1899\t//   - context: Additional context (optional)\n1900\t//   - responseSnippet: Excerpt from response (optional)\n1901\t//   - customSuggestions: Optional custom suggestions (if provided, these override auto-generated suggestions)\n1902\t//\n1903\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n1904\t//\n1905\t// Example usage with auto-generated suggestions:\n1906\t//\n1907\t//\terr := FormatValidationError(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"GET /api/users\", \"\")\n1908\t//\n1909\t// Example usage with custom suggestions:\n1910\t//\n1911\t//\terr := FormatValidationError(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"GET /api/users\", \"\",\n1912\t//\t    \"Check the endpoint documentation\", \"Verify the API version\")\n1913\tfunc FormatValidationError(validationType string, expected, actual interface{}, context, responseSnippet string, customSuggestions ...string) ValidationError {\n1914\t\tsuggestions := customSuggestions\n1915\t\tif len(customSuggestions) == 0 {\n1916\t\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(validationType, expected, actual)\n1917\t\t}\n1918\t\n1919\t\tve := ValidationError{\n1920\t\t\tErrorType:        validationType,\n1921\t\t\tMessage:          generateMessageFromParts(validationType, expected, actual),\n1922\t\t\tExpected:         expected,\n1923\t\t\tActual:           actual,\n1924\t\t\tContext:          context,\n1925\t\t\tResponseSnippet:  responseSnippet,\n1926\t\t\tSuggestions:      suggestions,\n1927\t\t}\n1928\t\n1929\t\treturn ve\n1930\t}\n1931\t\n1932\t// FormatValidationErrorWithDetails creates a ValidationError with extended field support.\n1933\t//\n1934\t// This function provides comprehensive control over validation error formatting with all\n1935\t// context fields, including optional fields for field names, locations, related fields,\n1936\t// pattern details, range information, validation details, and custom suggestions.\n1937\t//\n1938\t// # Features\n1939\t//\n1940\t//   - Complete field support: All ValidationError fields can be populated\n1941\t//   - Auto-generated suggestions: Smart suggestions based on validation type and values\n1942\t//   - Custom suggestions: Override auto-generated suggestions with domain-specific guidance\n1943\t//   - Flexible context: Add location, related fields, and validation-specific details\n1944\t//   - Response debugging: Include response snippets for troubleshooting\n1945\t//\n1946\t// # Parameters\n1947\t//\n1948\t// Core validation fields (required):\n1949\t//   - validationType: The validation category (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n1950\t//   - expected: The expected value (can be int, string, []int, etc.)\n1951\t//   - actual: The actual value received (can be int, string, []int, etc.)\n1952\t//\n1953\t// Context fields (optional but recommended):\n1954\t//   - context: Additional context about the validation operation (e.g., \"GET /api/users\", \"OAuth token validation\")\n1955\t//   - responseSnippet: Truncated excerpt from the response for debugging (recommended: 100-200 characters)\n1956\t//\n1957\t// Field-specific context (use when applicable):\n1958\t//   - fieldName: Specific field where error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"user.email\")\n1959\t//   - location: Where in the input the error occurred (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\", \"header 'Authorization'\")\n1960\t//   - relatedFields: Other fields related to this error (e.g., [\"email\", \"email_confirmation\"])\n1961\t//\n1962\t// Validation-specific details (use when applicable):\n1963\t//   - patternDetails: Pattern matching failure information (e.g., \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\")\n1964\t//   - rangeInfo: Range boundaries for range validations (e.g., \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\")\n1965\t//   - validationDetails: Additional validation-specific details as a list of strings\n1966\t//\n1967\t// Suggestions:\n1968\t//   - customSuggestions: Optional custom suggestions (variadic parameter; if provided, overrides auto-generated suggestions)\n1969\t//\n1970\t// # Returns\n1971\t//\n1972\t// A ValidationError struct populated with all provided fields and appropriate suggestions.\n1973\t//\n1974\t// # Auto-generated Suggestions\n1975\t//\n1976\t// Suggestions are automatically generated based on validationType and values:\n1977\t//\n1978\t//   - status_code: Context-aware suggestions based on specific HTTP status codes\n1979\t//     • 400: Check request syntax, required fields, content-type\n1980\t//     • 401: Verify credentials, token expiration, authorization format\n1981\t//     • 403: Check permissions, scopes, API documentation\n1982\t//     • 404: Verify endpoint URL, resource existence, deletion status\n1983\t//     • 409: Check for duplicates, unique constraints, use PUT vs POST\n1984\t//     • 429: Implement rate limiting, exponential backoff, check quotas\n1985\t//     • 5xx: Implement retry logic, check service status, contact support\n1986\t//\n1987\t//   - error_message: Pattern-based suggestions for common error scenarios\n1988\t//     • Token errors: Refresh, verify format, check revocation\n1989\t//     • Auth denied: Verify permissions, scopes, API key permissions\n1990\t//     • Not found: Check identifier, existence, deletion status\n1991\t//     • Validation: Review body, data types, field constraints\n1992\t//     • Rate/quota: Implement limiting, check quotas, add backoff\n1993\t//\n1994\t//   - content_type: Generic content-type troubleshooting suggestions\n1995\t//\n1996\t//   - status_code_range: Range-specific validation suggestions\n1997\t//\n1998\t//   - Other types: Generic troubleshooting suggestions\n1999\t//\n2000\t// # Example Usage\n2001\t//\n2002\t// Basic usage with auto-generated suggestions:\n2003\t//\n2004\t//\tve := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2005\t//\t    \"status_code\",\n2006\t//\t    200,\n2007\t//\t    404,\n2008\t//\t    \"GET /api/users/123\",\n2009\t//\t    \"\",\n2010\t//\t    \"\",\n2011\t//\t    \"\",\n2012\t//\t    nil,\n2013\t//\t    \"\",\n2014\t//\t    \"\",\n2015\t//\t    nil,\n2016\t//\t)\n2017\t//\t// Returns ValidationError with auto-generated suggestions for 404 errors:\n2018\t//\t// Suggestions: [\"Verify the endpoint URL is correct\", \"Check if the resource ID exists\", ...]\n2019\t//\n2020\t// Error message validation with field context:\n2021\t//\n2022\t//\tve := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2023\t//\t    \"error_message\",\n2024\t//\t    \"invalid.*token\",\n2025\t//\t    \"access_denied\",\n2026\t//\t    \"OAuth validation\",\n2027\t//\t    `{\"error\": \"access_denied\"}`,\n2028\t//\t    \"error\",\n2029\t//\t    \"field 'user.access_token'\",\n2030\t//\t    []string{\"user.access_token\", \"user.refresh_token\"},\n2031\t//\t    \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\",\n2032\t//\t    \"\",\n2033\t//\t    []string{\"No matching error field found\", \"Checked 3 error message fields\"},\n2034\t//\t)\n2035\t//\t// Returns ValidationError with field-specific context and pattern details\n2036\t//\n2037\t// Status code range validation with range info:\n2038\t//\n2039\t//\tve := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2040\t//\t    \"status_code_range\",\n2041\t//\t    \"4xx\",\n2042\t//\t    200,\n2043\t//\t    \"Error response check\",\n2044\t//\t    \"\",\n2045\t//\t    \"\",\n2046\t//\t    \"\",\n2047\t//\t    nil,\n2048\t//\t    \"\",\n2049\t//\t    \"400-499 (Client Error)\",\n2050\t//\t    []string{\"Status code 200 is outside expected range\"},\n2051\t//\t)\n2052\t//\t// Returns ValidationError with range information and validation details\n2053\t//\n2054\t// Content-Type validation with custom suggestions:\n2055\t//\n2056\t//\tve := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2057\t//\t    \"content_type\",\n2058\t//\t    \"application/json\",\n2059\t//\t    \"text/html\",\n2060\t//\t    \"POST /api/users\",\n2061\t//\t    `{\"status\": \"error\", \"message\": \"Invalid content type\"}`,\n2062\t//\t    \"Content-Type\",\n2063\t//\t    \"\",\n2064\t//\t    nil,\n2065\t//\t    \"\",\n2066\t//\t    \"\",\n2067\t//\t    nil,\n2068\t//\t    \"Set Content-Type header to application/json\",\n2069\t//\t    \"Ensure request body is valid JSON\",\n2070\t//\t    \"Check API documentation for required content type\",\n2071\t//\t)\n2072\t//\t// Returns ValidationError with custom suggestions overriding auto-generated ones\n2073\t//\n2074\t// Full context example with all optional fields:\n2075\t//\n2076\t//\tve := FormatValidationErrorWithDetails(\n2077\t//\t    \"error_message\",\n2078\t//\t    \"^User .* not found$\",\n2079\t//\t    \"Invalid credentials\",\n2080\t//\t    \"User lookup validation\",\n2081\t//\t    `{\"error\": \"Invalid credentials\", \"code\": \"AUTH_FAILED\"}`,\n2082\t//\t    \"message\",\n2083\t//\t    \"line 42 in user configuration\",\n2084\t//\t    []string{\"username\", \"password\", \"email\"},\n2085\t//\t    \"Expected pattern: ^User .* not found$, Actual: Invalid credentials\",\n2086\t//\t    \"\",\n2087\t//\t    []string{\n2088\t//\t        \"Pattern mismatch: expected 'User .* not found' format\",\n2089\t//\t        \"Actual message indicates authentication failure, not user not found\",\n2090\t//\t        \"This suggests a different endpoint or auth method was used\",\n2091\t//\t    },\n2092\t//\t)\n2093\t//\t// Returns ValidationError with comprehensive context for complex debugging\n2094\t//\n2095\t// # Field-Specific Usage Guidelines\n2096\t//\n2097\t// For validation types that benefit from field-specific context:\n2098\t//   - error_message: Use fieldName to specify the field (e.g., \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\")\n2099\t//   - response_structure: Use fieldName for the JSON path (e.g., \"data.items[0].id\")\n2100\t//   - content_type: Use fieldName for the header name (e.g., \"Content-Type\")\n2101\t//   - cors_headers: Use fieldName for specific CORS header (e.g., \"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n2102\t//\n2103\t// # Response Snippet Package validate provides helper functions for validating HTTP responses and other data.\npackage validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"regexp\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// HTTPStatusCodeIsValid checks if an HTTP response's status code matches one or more expected codes.\n// It supports both a single status code and a slice of valid status codes, making it useful for\n// validating API responses where multiple status codes might indicate success (e.g., 200 OK and 206 Partial Content).\n//\n// The expected parameter can be:\n//   - A single int (e.g., 200)\n//   - A slice of ints (e.g., []int{200, 201, 204})\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code matches any of the expected codes, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tsingleCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 200)\n//\tmultipleCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, []int{200, 201, 204})\n//\tnoContentCheck := HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(response, 204)\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected interface{}) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tstatusCode := resp.StatusCode\n\n\tswitch v := expected.(type) {\n\tcase int:\n\t\treturn statusCode == v\n\tcase []int:\n\t\tfor _, code := range v {\n\t\t\tif statusCode == code {\n\t\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn false\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n}\n\n// HTTPStatusCodeIsError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates an error (4xx or 5xx).\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code is in the 400-599 range, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsError(response) {\n//\t    // Handle error response\n//\t}\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 600\n}\n\n// HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates a client error (4xx).\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code is in the 400-499 range, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(response) {\n//\t    // Handle 4xx client error (bad request, unauthorized, etc.)\n//\t}\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsClientError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 400 && resp.StatusCode < 500\n}\n\n// HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError checks if an HTTP response's status code indicates a server error (5xx).\n//\n// Returns true if the response status code is in the 500-599 range, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError(response) {\n//\t    // Handle 5xx server error (internal server error, bad gateway, etc.)\n//\t}\nfunc HTTPStatusCodeIsServerError(resp *http.Response) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn resp.StatusCode >= 500 && resp.StatusCode < 600\n}\n\n// ContentTypeIsValid checks if a response's Content-Type header matches the expected pattern.\n// It supports pattern matching where the base content-type matches regardless of parameters.\n// For example, \"application/json\" will match \"application/json; charset=utf-8\".\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: The HTTP response to validate\n//   - expected: The expected content-type pattern (e.g., \"application/json\")\n//\n// Returns true if the response Content-Type matches the expected pattern, false otherwise.\n// Returns false if the response is nil or has no Content-Type header.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tif ContentTypeIsValid(response, \"application/json\") {\n//\t    // Handle JSON response\n//\t}\nfunc ContentTypeIsValid(resp *http.Response, expected string) bool {\n\tif resp == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tactual := resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif actual == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse both content-type strings to extract base media types\n\tactualMediaType := parseContentType(actual)\n\texpectedMediaType := parseContentType(expected)\n\n\treturn actualMediaType == expectedMediaType\n}\n\n// parseContentType extracts the base media type from a content-type string.\n// It strips parameters like charset, boundary, etc.\n// For example, \"application/json; charset=utf-8\" becomes \"application/json\".\nfunc parseContentType(contentType string) string {\n\tif contentType == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Split by semicolon to separate media type from parameters\n\t// The first part before any semicolon is the base media type\n\tfor i, c := range contentType {\n\t\tif c == ';' {\n\t\t\treturn trimSpace(contentType[:i])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// No parameters found, return the whole string trimmed\n\treturn trimSpace(contentType)\n}\n\n// trimSpace removes leading and trailing whitespace from a string.\nfunc trimSpace(s string) string {\n\tstart := 0\n\tend := len(s)\n\n\tfor start < end && (s[start] == ' ' || s[start] == '\\t' || s[start] == '\\n' || s[start] == '\\r') {\n\t\tstart++\n\t}\n\n\tfor end > start && (s[end-1] == ' ' || s[end-1] == '\\t' || s[end-1] == '\\n' || s[end-1] == '\\r') {\n\t\tend--\n\t}\n\n\treturn s[start:end]\n}\n\n// ErrorResponseFieldNames specifies custom field names to check when validating error response structures.\n// If nil, the default field names \"error\" and \"message\" are checked.\ntype ErrorResponseFieldNames struct {\n\t// PrimaryFieldName is the main error field to check (e.g., \"error\", \"detail\", \"error_description\")\n\tPrimaryFieldName string\n\t// SecondaryFieldName is an optional secondary field to check (e.g., \"message\", \"description\")\n\tSecondaryFieldName string\n}\n\n// DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames returns the default field names used for error response validation.\n// This checks for both \"error\" and \"message\" fields.\nfunc DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames() *ErrorResponseFieldNames {\n\treturn &ErrorResponseFieldNames{\n\t\tPrimaryFieldName:   \"error\",\n\t\tSecondaryFieldName: \"message\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrorResponseStructureIsValid validates whether a response object contains a valid error response structure.\n// It checks for the presence of common error fields and returns whether the structure is valid.\n//\n// The response parameter can be:\n//   - map[string]interface{}: A parsed JSON object representing the response body\n//   - Any type that can be inspected for error fields using reflection\n//\n// The fieldNames parameter allows customization of which fields to check:\n//   - If nil, checks for default fields \"error\" and \"message\"\n//   - If provided, checks for the specified primary and secondary field names\n//\n// Returns true if the response contains at least one of the expected error fields with a non-empty value,\n// false otherwise.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Using default field names (checks for \"error\" and \"message\")\n//\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\"error\": \"Invalid input\"}\n//\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, nil)\n//\n//\t// Using custom field names\n//\tcustomFields := &ErrorResponseFieldNames{PrimaryFieldName: \"detail\", SecondaryFieldName: \"description\"}\n//\tbody := map[string]interface{}{\"detail\": \"Resource not found\"}\n//\tisValid := ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(body, customFields)\nfunc ErrorResponseStructureIsValid(response interface{}, fieldNames *ErrorResponseFieldNames) bool {\n\tif response == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Use default field names if not provided\n\tif fieldNames == nil {\n\t\tfieldNames = DefaultErrorResponseFieldNames()\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to convert to map for field checking\n\trespMap, ok := response.(map[string]interface{})\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check primary
